content:
If you're playing through steam, and doing the purchase, the price is actually in HKD which gets converted by whatever rates steam uses, into your steam wallets currency.
There is also the official top up site, (https://shop.qookkagames.com/morimens/hk)
Where you can pay in usd, hkd, myr, sgd, and then the conversion rate is well.. up to your bank lol

Exactly tho, the price converted from hkd directly to cad should be 130$ cad for the most expensive silver bundle but its not, its 160$ cad, im saying that its over priced what it should be when converted to the region

I don't think(!!) the game has any influence on what rates steam, google or apple uses for the currency conversions? There are usually fees and such involved in those things too.
The best option, if the HKD->CAD rates you get from steam are bad, is probably their website, and praying that alipay(or the other payment options) or simply your bank via CC payment gives you a better rate C:

Obviously, I'm not an expert on how these things work, but generally, living in a non-€ country, I get absolutely garbage rates when purchasing things on appstore/gplay etc that try to charge me in my local currency, so i tend to be forced to use top-up websites where i can pay in USD/EUR, and use the rates of stripe/paypal/w/e, or my CC.

The auto conversion from within the game is higher for me as well (Google play).

I usually top up directly from their site instead.

1.5 (google) vs 1.3 (site)

https://shop.qookkagames.com/morimens/hk?from_op=blank&playerId=101143204
